stories.title: Home Run Derby Tonight — Harper-Schwarber Rematch, Netflix Debut
stories.angle: The history angle: Harper won here in 2018, Schwarber made the final that same night, Citizens Bank Park hasn't hosted the Derby since — and both Phillies are back in it. Plus the Netflix debut makes this feel genuinely new-era.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Home Run Derby is TONIGHT at Citizens Bank Park. 8 PM ET on Netflix.

Harper won here in 2018. Schwarber reached the final. Same ballpark, same two Phillies, different decade.

New format: 20/15/15 swings, no timer.

#Phillies #HomeRunDerby

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Two hours until the Home Run Derby.

Schwarber leads MLB with 30 HRs. Harper won this thing here in 2018.

Netflix, 8 PM ET. Citizens Bank Park.

One of these Phillies is walking away with the trophy tonight. ⚾

#Phillies #MLB

article.headline: The Home Run Derby Is Tonight: Harper, Schwarber, and the Rematch Nobody Forgot

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Home Run Derby Tonight

**Imagn Search Terms:**
- "Bryce Harper Philadelphia Phillies"
- "Kyle Schwarber home run"

**X Image (SB-2026-07-13-01A):**
- Photo: Harper or Schwarber mid-swing
- Text Overlay: "HOME RUN DERBY / TONIGHT 8 PM ET / NETFLIX / Citizens Bank Park"
- Sub-line: "Harper vs. Schwarber • 2018 Rematch"
- Brand: Ballpark Banter

**Facebook Image (SB-2026-07-13-01B):**
- Photo: Both Phillies (split-screen or stadium shot)
- Text Overlay: "WHO WINS TONIGHT?" bold at top
- Sub-line: "Home Run Derby • Citizens Bank Park • 8 PM ET Netflix"
- Brand: Ballpark Banter

---

research.facts:
Home Run Derby Tonight
**Sources consulted:** MLB.com, ESPN, Yahoo Sports, NBC Sports, Netflix
**Confirmed facts:**
- **[HIGH]** 2026 Home Run Derby: Tonight, July 13, 2026, 8 PM ET, Citizens Bank Park, Philadelphia
- **[HIGH]** First time the Home Run Derby has aired on Netflix; MLB on Netflix debut
- **[HIGH]** 8-player confirmed field: Bryce Harper (PHI), Kyle Schwarber (PHI), Junior Caminero (TB), Munetaka Murakami (CWS), Jordan Walker (STL), Ben Rice (NYY), Jac Caglianone (KC), Willson Contreras (BOS)
- **[HIGH]** Format: Round 1 = 20 swings, Round 2 = 15 swings, Final = 15 swings; no timer; if a player hits a HR on their final swing, they keep swinging until they don't; ties settled by 3-swing swing-off
- **[HIGH]** Schwarber leads MLB with 30 HRs through 82 games; reigning NL HR leader (56 HRs last season)
- **[HIGH]** Harper-Schwarber rematch: In 2018 Derby at Citizens Bank Park, Schwarber reached the final and lost to Harper
- **[HIGH]** Harper won 2018 Derby at Citizens Bank Park; seeking to become first player ever to win Derby twice in home ballpark
- **[HIGH]** Junior Caminero (age 23 on game day); would be youngest Derby champion ever if he wins, surpassing Juan Gonzalez (1993) — LOW confidence on Gonzalez record (unverified secondary claim)
- **[MEDIUM]** Top 4 from Round 1 advance; head-to-head matchups in Round 2; winners advance to Final
**Sources:** MLB.com (Derby participants), ESPN (bracket analysis), Yahoo Sports (field preview), NBC Sports

stories.title: Justin Verlander Announces Retirement — 3 Cy Youngs, 266 Wins, 21 Seasons
stories.angle: The three-decades Cy Young arc is rare. He won in his prime (2011), his second act (2019), and his renaissance after Tommy John surgery (2022). The 10th All-Star as Legend Pick is the perfect curtain call.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Justin Verlander is retiring after 2026.

266 wins. 3,554 Ks. 3 Cy Youngs. 21 seasons. 10 All-Star Games.

He won them in 2011, 2019, and 2022 — three different decades.

He started in Detroit. He's ending in Detroit.

#Tigers #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Verlander's 2011 season: AL MVP (unanimous), Pitching Triple Crown.

He had Tommy John surgery at 35 and came back to win a third Cy Young at 39.

That's the career you're saying goodbye to. 🐯

#Tigers

article.headline: Three Cy Youngs. Three Decades. Justin Verlander Is Retiring.

research.facts:
Justin Verlander Retirement
**Sources consulted:** MLB.com, ESPN, CBS Sports, Yahoo Sports, Detroit News, Baseball Reference
**Confirmed facts:**
- **[HIGH]** Verlander announced retirement at end of 2026 season; announced approximately July 8-9, 2026
- **[HIGH]** Quote: "I've realized that time has come."
- **[HIGH]** On 60-day IL with hip and hamstring injuries
- **[HIGH]** Commissioner Manfred named him AL "Legend Pick" for 10th All-Star Game
- **[HIGH]** Will finish career with Detroit Tigers, where it began
- **[HIGH]** Career record: 266-159, 3.33 ERA, 3,554 career strikeouts
- **[HIGH]** 21 MLB seasons
- **[HIGH]** 3 Cy Young Awards: 2011, 2019, 2022 (not 2004 — confirmed correction)
- **[HIGH]** 1 AL MVP Award: 2011 (unanimous; also won Pitching Triple Crown that year)
- **[HIGH]** 2 World Series championships
- **[HIGH]** 10 All-Star selections
**Sources:** MLB.com, ESPN, CBS Sports, Fox News, Detroit News, MLB Trade Rumors, Yahoo Sports

stories.title: All-Star Game Tomorrow — Ohtani and Judge Both Out
stories.angle: The Ohtani-Judge void creates a "who steps up?" storyline. The USA 250 angle is distinctive. Verlander's Legend Pick appearance is the emotional center. This is a preview-and-context piece.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
All-Star Game is tomorrow. July 14. Citizens Bank Park. FOX. 8 PM ET.

Shohei Ohtani: out (knee).
Aaron Judge: out (fractured rib, out since late May).

The two biggest stars in baseball won't be there.

Someone else gets a chance to own the moment. #MLB #AllStar

article.headline: Ohtani Is Out. Judge Is Out. The All-Star Game Is Tomorrow.

research.facts:
All-Star Game Tomorrow
**Sources consulted:** Front Office Sports, Fox News, Yahoo Sports, MLB.com, HITC
**Confirmed facts:**
- **[HIGH]** 96th MLB All-Star Game: July 14, 2026, 8 PM ET, FOX and FOX One, Citizens Bank Park Philadelphia
- **[HIGH]** First All-Star Game ever at Citizens Bank Park (5th in Philadelphia, 4th hosted by Phillies)
- **[HIGH]** USA 250 theme: 250th anniversary of signing of Declaration of Independence
- **[HIGH]** Special patriotic jerseys: red, white, blue, stars-and-stripes graphics, America 250 branding
- **[HIGH]** Shohei Ohtani out: left knee irritation; fluid drained from knee; pain when pitching; skipped final start before break
- **[HIGH]** Aaron Judge out: fractured rib; out since late May; using break to have rib re-imaged
- **[HIGH]** Front Office Sports described joint absence as "a sizable blow for MLB" / potential ratings hit for FOX
**Sources:** Front Office Sports, Fox News, The Big Lead, Yahoo Sports, HITC, Yardbarker

stories.title: Tarik Skubal — "Zero Appetite to Be Traded" Before August 3 Deadline
stories.angle: The tension: his own feelings vs. the business case for the Tigers to move him. A two-time Cy Young in a contract year, on a bad team, 3 weeks from the deadline. This is the most dramatic trade saga of the break.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Tarik Skubal on trade rumors: "zero appetite to be traded."

Two-time reigning AL Cy Young. Contract year. Extension talks with Detroit: nil. Yankees and Phillies already circling.

August 3 deadline: 21 days away. #MLB #Tigers

article.headline: Tarik Skubal Says He Has "Zero Appetite to Be Traded." The Tigers Have Three Weeks.

research.facts:
Tarik Skubal Trade Rumors
**Sources consulted:** Motor City Bengals, Bleacher Report, Yahoo Sports, Audacy/971 The Ticket
**Confirmed facts:**
- **[HIGH]** Skubal is two-time reigning AL Cy Young winner (2024 and 2025)
- **[HIGH]** 2024 Cy Young: unanimous (all 30 first-place votes), also captured Pitching Triple Crown
- **[HIGH]** 2025 stats: 13-6, 2.21 ERA, 241 Ks, 0.891 WHIP
- **[HIGH]** 2026 salary: $32 million (won arbitration hearing February 5, 2026)
- **[HIGH]** Contract year; expected to seek at least $400 million in free agency
- **[HIGH]** Extension talks with Tigers described by league insider as "nil"
- **[HIGH]** Quote: Skubal "badly wants to stay in Detroit the rest of the season, believing they have a legitimate shot at the World Series, and has zero appetite to be traded"
- **[HIGH]** Yankees and Phillies described as early leaders in potential Skubal sweepstakes
- **[HIGH]** MLB trade deadline: August 3, 2026
- **[MEDIUM]** Tigers sit near bottom of AL standings (specific record not confirmed in search)
**Sources:** Motor City Bengals, Bleacher Report, Yahoo Sports (Cy Young 2025), Audacy/971 The Ticket

stories.title: Futures Game Recap + White Sox Surprise
stories.angle: The White Sox are the sleeper story of MLB's first half: a team everyone wrote off that is actually competing. Futures Game + Murakami in the Derby gives an entry point.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
AL 6, NL 1. Nathan Flewelling (Rays) wins Futures Game MVP with a 365-foot 2-run HR.

The White Sox rebuild quietly: 48-45, leading the AL Central. 5 prospects in the Top 100. Roch Cholowsky No. 1 overall this year.

Everyone wrote them off. Nobody told them. #WhiteSox #MLB

article.headline: AL 6, NL 1: Nathan Flewelling Wins the Futures Game MVP — and the White Sox Deserve a Closer Look

research.facts:
Futures Game + White Sox Rebuild
**Sources consulted:** MLB.com, NBC Sports, CBS Sports, South Side Sox, Sox On 35th, Baseball America, Washington Times, Bleacher Report
**Confirmed facts:**
- **[HIGH]** 2026 MLB Futures Game: July 12, 2026, Citizens Bank Park, Philadelphia
- **[HIGH]** Final score: AL 6, NL 1
- **[HIGH]** MVP: Nathan Flewelling, C, Tampa Bay Rays; third Rays prospect to win the award
- **[HIGH]** Flewelling: 365-foot, 2-run HR in 6th inning to extend AL lead to 4-1
- **[HIGH]** Scoring: AL scored on Franklin Arias groundout (De Vries RBI); NL tied on Jesus Made RBI (Edward Florentino scored); AL retook lead on Theo Gillen RBI single (throwing error); Flewelling 2-run HR; JoJo Parker RBI double (Kevin Alvarez scored); Ralphy Velazquez RBI single (Parker scored)
- **[HIGH]** White Sox 2026 record: 48-45, leading AL Central (surprising given rebuild expectations)
- **[HIGH]** 2026 MLB Draft: White Sox selected Roch Cholowsky, SS, UCLA, with the No. 1 overall pick
- **[HIGH]** 5 White Sox prospects in MLB Pipeline 2026 Top 100 (tied for 4th most in MLB with Dodgers, Marlins, Pirates, Cardinals): Braden Montgomery OF (#36), Noah Schultz LHP (#49), Caleb Bonemer INF (#61), Hagen Smith LHP (#72), Billy Carlson SS (#73)
- **[HIGH]** Munetaka Murakami (White Sox rookie) competing in tonight's Home Run Derby
**Sources:** MLB.com, NBC Sports, Washington Times, Bleacher Report, Baseball America, Yahoo Sports, South Side Sox, Sox On 35th
